proven.claim: PROVEN: contractsDir exists when execution reaches line 115.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 115
; PROVEN: contractsDir exists when execution reaches line 115.
; The guard `if (!existsSync(this.contractsDir)) return` eliminates the false path.

(declare-const contractsDir_exists Int)  ; 1 = dir exists, 0 = does not
(declare-const at_line_115 Int)          ; 1 = execution reaches line 115

; Code transition: early return fires when dir does not exist
(assert (=> (= contractsDir_exists 0) (= at_line_115 0)))

; Given: we ARE at line 115 (the log executes)
(assert (= at_line_115 1))

; Negation of property: contractsDir does not exist
(assert (= contractsDir_exists 0))

(check-sat)

proven.claim: PROVEN: contracts.size >= 0 — Map.set only adds, never subtracts.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 115
; PROVEN: contracts.size >= 0 — Map.set only adds, never subtracts.
; Degenerate lower bound is 0 (all parses fail), not negative.

(declare-const successful_sets Int)  ; number of Map.set() calls that completed
(declare-const contracts_size Int)   ; Map.size at line 115

; Code transition: each completed Map.set() adds one entry (or overwrites)
; Map.size is bounded below by 0
(assert (>= successful_sets 0))
; Code transition: Map.size cannot exceed successful_sets (keys can collide)
(assert (= contracts_size successful_sets))

; Negation: contracts.size < 0
(assert (< contracts_size 0))

(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: contracts.size = 0 is reachable even when .json files exist on disk.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 115
; VIOLATION: contracts.size = 0 is reachable even when .json files exist on disk.
; Empty catch blocks silently swallow all parse errors; no file produces a set() call.
; The log then claims "Loaded 0 contracts from disk" — a degenerate, misleading state.

(declare-const files_found Int)      ; .json files returned by walk()
(declare-const parse_successes Int)  ; files that produced valid JSON with key/contracts
(declare-const contracts_size Int)   ; Map.size at line 115

; Code transition: walk() found at least one .json file
(assert (> files_found 0))

; Code transition: each parse is inside try { } catch {}; all can fail silently
(assert (>= parse_successes 0))
(assert (<= parse_successes files_found))

; Code transition: all parse attempts fail — catch {} swallows every error
(assert (= parse_successes 0))

; Code transition: no Map.set() calls → Map.size = 0
(assert (= contracts_size parse_successes))

; Violation: log prints "Loaded 0 contracts" while files_found > 0
(assert (= contracts_size 0))
(assert (> files_found 0))

(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: JSON.parse output is used without schema validation.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 115
; VIOLATION: JSON.parse output is used without schema validation.
; If data.proven is null/undefined, contract.proven.map() throws at runtime.
; The throw is caught by the outer catch {}, so this.contracts.set() never executes.
; The contract is silently dropped; contracts.size undercounts.

(declare-const has_valid_key Int)     ; 1 = data.key is truthy
(declare-const proven_is_array Int)   ; 1 = data.proven is a valid Array
(declare-const set_executes Int)      ; 1 = this.contracts.set() is called
(declare-const contracts_size Int)

; Code transition: data.key is truthy → enter if(data.key) branch
(assert (= has_valid_key 1))

; Code transition: JSON.parse makes no schema guarantees — proven can be null
; (TypeScript's "as const" cast does not validate at runtime)
(assert (= proven_is_array 0))

; Code transition: .map() called on null throws TypeError
;   → outer catch {} fires → set() never called for this file
(assert (=> (= proven_is_array 0) (= set_executes 0)))
(assert (= set_executes 0))

; Code transition: no set() call → this contract absent from map
(assert (= contracts_size 0))

; Violation: file had data.key set (should have been loaded) but is silently dropped
(assert (= has_valid_key 1))
(assert (= contracts_size 0))

(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: Key collision — two .json files contain contracts sharing the same key.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 115
; VIOLATION: Key collision — two .json files contain contracts sharing the same key.
; Map.set() silently overwrites the first with the second.
; contracts.size is smaller than the number of contracts successfully parsed.
; The log claim "Loaded N contracts" hides the silent data loss.

(declare-const total_set_calls Int)   ; total Map.set() calls across all files
(declare-const key_collisions Int)    ; calls where key already existed in map
(declare-const contracts_size Int)    ; Map.size = distinct keys after all sets

; Code transition: at least 2 contracts successfully parsed and set
(assert (>= total_set_calls 2))

; Code transition: at least one key appears in two different files
(assert (> key_collisions 0))
(assert (<= key_collisions (- total_set_calls 1)))

; Code transition: Map.set() with a duplicate key overwrites silently
; Map.size = total_set_calls - key_collisions (second write does not grow size)
(assert (= contracts_size (- total_set_calls key_collisions)))

; Violation: logged size strictly less than contracts parsed — data silently lost
(assert (< contracts_size total_set_calls))

(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: A single .json file with data.contracts=[c1,...,cN] adds N entries.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 115
; VIOLATION: A single .json file with data.contracts=[c1,...,cN] adds N entries.
; contracts.size is not monotonically related to files_found.
; "Loaded N contracts from disk" is semantically ambiguous — N can far exceed
; the number of files, or be 0, giving no useful signal about disk state.

(declare-const files_found Int)              ; .json files returned by walk()
(declare-const contracts_in_batch Int)       ; entries in a single data.contracts array
(declare-const contracts_size Int)           ; Map.size at line 115

; Code transition: exactly 1 .json file found
(assert (= files_found 1))

; Code transition: that file takes the data.contracts branch with N > 1 entries
(assert (> contracts_in_batch 1))

; Code transition: each entry calls Map.set() independently
; (all entries distinct keys — no collision in this scenario)
(assert (= contracts_size contracts_in_batch))

; Violation: contracts.size > files_found — log gives no indication of disk structure
; The same log format is emitted whether 1 file held 100 contracts or 100 files held 1 each
(assert (> contracts_size files_found))

(check-sat)
